results.summary: Reworked TimestampMillis::now() to use u64::try_from(d.as_millis()).unwrap_or(u64::MAX) (no silent u128->u64 wrap) and added fallible try_now() -> Result<Self, SystemTimeError> that surfaces the duration_since error; now() delegates to try_now and saturates to 0 only on pre-epoch clocks. Added unit test.
results.notes: Imported std::time::SystemTimeError.

results.summary: Replaced raw substring protected-path gate in evaluate_delegated_task_runtime_readiness with delegated_target_matches_protected_pattern, which normalizes both / and \ separators into lowercased components and matches the protected pattern as a contiguous component-window run (boundary-aware). Patterns with no usable path components fall back to a case-insensitive substring check so opaque labels still fail closed. Added unit tests covering boundary match and cross-boundary rejection (e.g. .gitignore vs .git).
results.notes: Component-aware matching done in-memory (no filesystem canonicalize) since target_path_labels are display-safe labels, not real paths.

results.summary: In handle_transport_envelope, added authorization checks: Operation payloads require envelope.sender_participant_id == operation.author_participant_id and Presence payloads require envelope.sender_participant_id == projection.participant_id, returning InvalidOperation on mismatch before dispatch. Added negative tests for operation- and presence-sender spoofing plus a positive matching-sender test.

In the participant roster loop, the insert into participant_map is now checked with .is_some() and returns InvalidParticipant on a duplicate id, preventing silent overwrite of principal/permissions. Added duplicate_participant_id_is_rejected test.
results.notes: With duplicates rejected, the deduplicated map equals submitted count so the existing max_participants bound remains correct.

results.summary: submit_operation now iterates operation.preconditions.base_vector.entries before acceptance and, for any entry where participant_sequences.get(entry.participant_id) < entry.sequence, emits a CollaborationCausalGap and a GapDetected acknowledgement instead of replaying. Added base_vector_dependency_on_unseen_sequence_emits_gap test.

Implemented conservative fail-closed: a destructive (Delete/Replace) operation that is concurrent with an already-accepted operation it did not observe (via has_concurrent_accepted_operation, the negation of depends_on) is now rejected with a ResyncRequired acknowledgement instead of applying a raw, possibly-wrong-but-in-bounds byte range. Inserts remain accepted (non-destructive, convergent under the deterministic total order; out-of-bounds/non-boundary cases already fail-closed via replace_range). Updated the existing delete_replace test to declare its real causal dependency and added concurrent_destructive_operation_is_fail_closed.
results.notes: Remaining work: a real OT/CRDT inclusion-transformation step that adjusts byte ranges of concurrent operations during replay (so concurrent destructive edits can be safely applied rather than rejected), and positional transform of concurrent inserts so they target correct offsets rather than relying on deterministic total-order convergence. The exploit (silent document corruption from concurrent destructive ops) is closed.

results.summary: Replaced DefaultHasher in metadata_hash with a self-contained, deterministic SHA-256 (pure-Rust FIPS 180-4 impl, since no sha2/blake3 dep is available and Cargo.toml is out of scope) emitting the full 64-char hex digest. metadata_fingerprint now sets algorithm to 'sha256:<domain>' and domain-separates the hashed input so identical bytes under different domains never collide. Added golden/stability tests (empty/abc/hello vectors) and a fingerprint domain-separation test.
results.notes: Used a vendored pure-Rust SHA-256 because adding sha2/blake3 would require editing Cargo.toml (forbidden). Output verified against published SHA-256 test vectors.

results.summary: Converted the MetadataOnly/None redaction branch to allowlist-based via new redact_metadata_value/is_safe_metadata_key helpers (replacing is_metadata_value). Non-string scalars pass through; free-form strings are collapsed to 'hash=<digest>;len=<n>' unless the key is an allowlisted classifier/id/hash key AND the value contains no forbidden marker (contains_forbidden_assisted_ai_marker). Arrays/objects become '<metadata-only>'. Added two tests covering free-form hashing and forbidden-marker rejection on an allowlisted key.

assert_core_ids remains in place; it is already fail-closed (panics on nil causality / zero correlation / zero sequence, preventing emission of invalid events). Did NOT convert the ~16 public envelope helpers to Result.
results.notes: Converting the helpers to Result would ripple to legion-app (imports proposal_applied_event, proposal_approved_event, save_denied_event, proposal_audit_recorded_event, etc.; call site e.g. crates/legion-app/src/lib.rs:10480) which is out of scope per the edit-only-legion-observability restriction. Per the rules I preferred leaving the existing fail-closed behavior over a cross-crate-breaking signature change.

results.summary: Added a fail-closed invariant guard at the top of proposal_audit_record: assert_eq!(transition.proposal_id, proposal.proposal_id), so a mismatched proposal/transition pairing can no longer silently produce a corrupted audit record. Added accept-matching and should_panic mismatch tests.
results.notes: The recipe's Result<ProposalAuditRecord, ObservabilityError> signature change would ripple to legion-app (proposal_audit_record call at crates/legion-app/src/lib.rs:10337), which is out of scope. Used an assert (consistent with the module's existing assert_core_ids invariant style) for fail-closed rejection without changing the public signature.

results.summary: Rewrote NativeProcessService::execute to spawn with piped stdin/stdout/stderr instead of blocking command.output(). stdout/stderr are drained on dedicated reader threads (avoids pipe-buffer deadlock); when request.timeout is set, the call polls child.try_wait() against started.elapsed() and on overrun calls terminate_timed_out_process (SIGKILL to the child's process group on Unix via a new setpgid pre_exec, then child.kill()/wait()) and returns PlatformError::Timeout. Added test process_execution_enforces_timeout_while_child_runs asserting a 10s child is aborted within 5s with a Timeout error.
results.notes: Unix-cfg portions (setpgid pre_exec, group SIGKILL) not compiled on this Windows host; cross-platform timeout path and test verified on Windows.

results.summary: PtyRequest (crates/legion-platform/src/lib.rs:698-706) has only command/args/cwd fields - there is no request.env to forward, so child_environment_vars(&[]) is not actually dropping per-request env. Forwarding env would require adding a public env field to PtyRequest, which ripples to its struct-literal construction in crates/legion-terminal/src/lib.rs:555 (out of scope; cannot add a required field without editing that crate).
results.notes: To truly fix, add `env: Vec<(String,String)>` to PtyRequest and forward it, plus update the legion-terminal construction site - a cross-crate change.

results.summary: In kill_pty (Unix branch) added PtyKillMode::Terminate to the negative-PID (process-group) target set so close_pty's SIGTERM reaches the whole group (child is session leader via setsid), not just the leader. After the 500ms wait, if the child has not exited, escalate with SIGKILL to the process group and wait/reap again before removing the session entry; the error message now reflects the SIGKILL escalation.
results.notes: Unix-cfg code; not compiled on this Windows host. Follows the existing wait_unix_child_exit/nix patterns in the file.

results.summary: Replaced list_directory's filter_map(|entry| entry.ok()) with an explicit loop that propagates per-entry errors via PlatformError::from_io_error("list directory", path, err), so failed read_dir entries surface instead of being silently dropped. Added test list_directory_lists_entries_sorted.

handle_filesystem_operation and handle_process_descriptor now reject when the payload's embedded operation_id != envelope.operation_id (added `|| operation.operation_id != operation_id` and `|| descriptor.operation_id != operation_id`), returning RemoteRuntimeError::InvalidOperation. session_id was already checked; these are the only payload fields carrying an embedded operation_id, so no other consistency fields apply.

validate_mutation_gate now calls validate_capability(&preconditions.capability_decision, "remote.fs.write") (denies on error) and compares preconditions.principal_id against self.descriptor.authority.principal_id (denies on mismatch) before proceeding.
results.notes: Correlation/causality binding to the envelope is implicitly covered: write_preconditions travel inside the RemoteFilesystemOperation payload which is itself the envelope unit, and Finding 7 now binds operation.operation_id == envelope.operation_id so preconditions cannot be replayed under a different envelope. The two concretely exploitable gaps named in the evidence (missing capability-name validation and missing principal match) are closed.

results.summary: create_file now computes fingerprint: metadata_fingerprint(&content) from the actual built content instead of the constant string, matching seed_file/write_file.

Added derived_snapshot_id(path, content_version, operation_id) = SnapshotId(stable_hash_u128(path:cv:op) | 1). write_file, create_file, and rename_file now assign a fresh post-mutation snapshot id (instead of reusing preconditions.snapshot_id or leaving the renamed entry unchanged), returned via snapshot_for_path.

common_headers now returns Result<HeaderMap, RemoteRuntimeError>; invalid AUTHORIZATION or X-Legion-Client-Identity HeaderValue::from_str errors map to RemoteRuntimeError::InvalidOperation and propagate via send_with_body (self.common_headers()?) instead of being silently dropped.

Added encode_path_segment (RFC 3986 pchar allowlist) applied to task_id.0 in all four HTTP URL builders (events/cancel/proposal/evidence) so `/ ? #` and control bytes are percent-encoded and cannot alter the path/query. Also tightened validate_cloud_task_id to reject ids containing control chars or `/ \ ? #`.
results.notes: pchar set (unreserved + sub-delims + ':' + '@') is passed through so legitimate colon-delimited ids like cloud-task:http:1 remain unchanged; no percent_encoding crate dependency was added (would require Cargo.toml edit, out of scope).

results.summary: serve_one now reads via read_full_http_request: accumulates until the \r\n\r\n header terminator, parses Content-Length, then reads the full body, with a 5s read timeout set on the accepted stream. Replaces the single fixed 4096-byte read that could truncate segmented/large requests.

The three before-network rejection tests (disabled_policy, forbidden_upload, cost_cap) no longer spawn a listener; they use unreachable_base_url() (http://127.0.0.1:1) so no accept() thread is leaked and any accidental network attempt fails fast. Added a read timeout to serve_one's accepted stream for genuine-listener tests.
results.notes: Genuine-listener tests self-terminate after serving one request, so a join/shutdown handle was not added; the leak source (listeners that were never contacted) is fully removed.

results.summary: Changed accepted_sequences from VecDeque<EventSequence> to VecDeque<(EventSequence, RemoteOperationId)> in try_accept_frame so that when the replay window slides (pop_front), the evicted operation id is also removed from seen_operations. This bounds seen_operations to replay_window_size, so replay_window().accepted_operation_count (which reports seen_operations.len()) and audit_summary are no longer unbounded. inflight_operations remains separate (removed on ack). Updated replay_window front() access for the new tuple type. Added test remote_transport_state_machine_bounds_seen_operations_to_replay_window.
results.notes: Out-of-window operations are now caught by the contiguous forward-only frame_sequence check (ReplayRejected) rather than reported as Duplicate, which is the expected behavior for a bounded replay window.

results.summary: Added two validation guards in checkpoint() before assigning self.last_checkpoint: reject with ReplayRejected when checkpoint.event_sequence.0 > self.last_sequence.0 (ahead of accepted frames), and reject when it is below the lowest retained accepted_sequence (outside the replay window). Added test remote_transport_state_machine_rejects_out_of_window_checkpoint_sequence covering ahead, stale, and valid-in-window cases.
results.notes: Used a let-chain to satisfy clippy::collapsible_if.

results.summary: Lease expiry was set to TimestampMillis(policy.ttl_ms), treating a duration as an absolute epoch. Now computed in both capture_descriptor and capture_bundle as TimestampMillis(now_ms.saturating_add(policy.ttl_ms).min(grant.expires_at.0)) using TimestampMillis::now(), so it is an absolute timestamp clamped to the consent grant.
results.notes: Existing purge_expired test still passes: grant.expires_at (60_000) clamps the lease, keeping its recorded value, while real captures now use wall-clock TTL.

results.summary: pack_files now accumulates raw file byte total and returns RawSourceVaultError::Denied once it exceeds request.max_bytes, enforcing the consent-bound budget instead of only the vault config limit. Added test file_backed_vault_rejects_capture_exceeding_consent_byte_budget.

flush_index now writes to index.json.tmp, fsyncs the file, renames over index.json, and best-effort fsyncs the parent dir (ignored where unsupported, e.g. Windows). open() now quarantines a corrupt index to index.json.corrupt-<ts> and fails closed instead of erroring without preservation or silently starting empty.

capture_bundle now writes ciphertext to a temp .vault.tmp file, stages index mutations in memory, persists the index via flush_index, and only then renames temp->bundle_path. On flush failure it reverts the staged index entries (new revert_bundle_index helper) and removes the temp file; on rename failure it reverts, re-flushes, and removes the temp file, so no orphaned or dangling files remain.

delete_bundle now treats NotFound from fs::remove_file as recoverable (proceeds to drop index entries and record the tombstone) and only errors on other IO failures. purge_expired now continues on per-bundle delete failures (counting only successes) instead of aborting on the first failure. Updated the prior test to assert delete now succeeds and removes the descriptor when ciphertext is missing.
results.notes: Renamed test file_backed_vault_delete_fails_when_ciphertext_is_missing -> file_backed_vault_delete_tolerates_missing_ciphertext to match the corrected fail-safe behavior.

results.summary: Targets crates/legion-sandbox/src/landlock.rs (LandlockProfile). That symbol does not exist anywhere in legion-security; it belongs to legion-sandbox, a different (non-workspace-member) package outside my scope.
results.notes: Real location is legion-sandbox, which has no Cargo.toml and is not compiled; not editable from this package.

results.summary: Targets crates/legion-sandbox/src/seatbelt.rs (SeatbeltProfile). Symbol absent from legion-security; belongs to legion-sandbox.
results.notes: Different package, out of scope.

results.summary: Targets crates/legion-sandbox/src/windows.rs (WindowsProfile). Symbol absent from legion-security; belongs to legion-sandbox.
results.notes: Different package, out of scope.

results.summary: Targets windows.rs `documented_fallback` in legion-sandbox. No such field/file in legion-security.
results.notes: Different package, out of scope.

results.summary: Reworked NormalizedPolicyPath::starts_with to require symmetric prefix equality (self.prefix != root.prefix => false). The default "./" roots (prefix None, no segments) no longer match absolute candidates, so can_access("/tmp/file", Read) now returns false while workspace-relative access still works. Added regression test default_path_policy_does_not_allow_absolute_paths.
results.notes: Chose symmetric-prefix check over the recipe's empty-root guard because the latter would deny all relative workspace access (breaking the intended default); symmetric check fixes the absolute-path bypass without that regression and matches the recipe's stated intent.

results.summary: Rewrote the fs.* arm: fs.write with no target_path now denies (was allow); fs.read/fs.list now require a path and run PathPolicy::can_access (Read/List); unknown fs.* subcommands deny-by-default. Added tests fs_read_requires_path_and_honors_path_policy and fs_write_without_path_fails_closed.
results.notes: Only fs.read/fs.write are used in the codebase; list handled defensively, all other fs.* denied.

results.summary: remote.fs.read/remote.fs.write now require a target path and enforce PathPolicy::can_access (Read/Write, including blocked-root checks); missing path fails closed. Threaded `path` into remote_capability_decision. Updated remote_policy_allows_filesystem_without_execution test and added a missing-path deny assertion.
results.notes: CapabilityRequestContext has no dedicated remote-path field, so the request target_path (already plumbed as `path`) is used as the canonical remote path.

results.summary: CommandTaxonomy::classify now parses git subcommands (push/fetch/pull/clone/remote=>Network; clean/checkout/reset/commit/etc=>Mutate; else Read) instead of keying only on the first token. terminal.launch now denies Network/Mutate/Unknown launch binaries (kept Terminal/LanguageServer/Read). cmd.* arm and requires_trusted_workspace_for_request now treat Unknown as deny/trusted-required for untrusted workspaces. Added tests git_subcommands_are_classified_by_verb and cmd_git_push_is_denied_for_untrusted_workspace.
results.notes: Deviated from the recipe's literal 'deny Terminal at terminal.launch': launching a shell (Terminal class) is the terminal's legitimate purpose and an existing test asserts bash=>Allow, so Terminal stays allowed while the real bypass (git push as Read, unknown/mutate/network launch binaries) is closed.

results.summary: lsp.launch now requires context.lsp_server_binary, rejects binaries not in lsp_policy.allowed_binaries, denies network-refresh launches when deny_network_refresh is set, and the catch-all now denies unknown lsp.* subcommands instead of allowing them. Added test lsp_launch_requires_allowlisted_binary.
results.notes: Used the actual context field lsp_server_binary (recipe said command_binary, which is the terminal field).

results.summary: CapabilityBrokerPort::handle no longer echoes caller-supplied Grant/Deny envelopes as authoritative Granted/Denied responses; both now return Err(ProtocolError::validation(..)). Only Request envelopes go through the policy engine. Added test broker_rejects_grant_and_deny_envelopes.
results.notes: Fail-closed: external Grant/Deny rejected rather than trusted.

results.summary: Moved the decision counter to AtomicU64 with a manual Clone impl and a next_decision_id() helper. handle() no longer clones the broker; it increments the real instance's counter via &self, so generated CapabilityDecisionIds are monotonic across repeated calls. Added test handle_generates_monotonic_decision_ids (asserts ids 1,2,3).
results.notes: Public decide/decide_with_request_context kept as &mut self to avoid unused_mut churn across ~30 existing tests; atomic works fine under &mut self.

results.summary: policy.rs was missing from disk/git (crate did not compile). Recreated it with ProposalAutoApprovalPolicy::allows_rule_ids implemented fail-closed: returns false when disabled or when rule_ids is empty, and requires every id to be non-empty and present in allowed_rule_ids. Also provided BatchRuntimeApplyPolicy and ProposalApplyGate (fail-closed defaults) required by the lib.rs re-export. Added unit tests including empty_rule_ids_are_never_auto_approved.
results.notes: Module was referenced by lib.rs:24-25 but never existed; recreating it was required to make the package compile and directly implements the requested !rule_ids.is_empty() guard.

results.summary: risk.rs was missing from disk/git. Recreated it with is_dependency_or_lockfile covering Rust (Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock), npm/yarn/pnpm/deno/bun, Go, Python, Ruby, PHP, and JVM manifests + lockfiles, name-compared case-insensitively across / and \\ separators. Added unit test manifests_and_lockfiles_are_detected.
results.notes: Module referenced by lib.rs:26 but never existed; recreated to compile and to implement the requested manifest coverage. Self-contained (no current consumers in the crate).

results.summary: In the recreated risk.rs, path_scope_risk treats an absent/blank workspace_root as RiskLevel::Deny (never low-risk), and returns Elevated for paths escaping the root or containing '..'. Added unit test missing_workspace_root_is_not_low_risk.
results.notes: Missing scope metadata is no longer treated as low-risk evidence, per the finding.

results.summary: Added a fail-closed equality check at the start of FileBackedTelemetrySpool::pending_batch that rejects with TelemetrySpoolError::InvalidMetadata when the passed endpoint != consent.endpoint (full struct equality, stricter than the recipe's field list). Since export_once delegates to pending_batch, both upload paths are now covered. Added test pending_batch_rejects_endpoint_not_matching_consent.
results.notes: HostedTelemetryEndpointDescriptor derives PartialEq/Eq in legion-protocol, so direct != comparison is valid and includes endpoint_id, label, region, allowlisted, schema_version.

results.summary: Added SUPPORTED_SPOOL_SCHEMA_VERSION const and a validate_persisted_spool helper invoked in FileBackedTelemetrySpool::open after JSON decode. It rejects unsupported schema_version, records.len() > config.max_records, and runs validate_hosted_telemetry_spool_record on every decoded record, returning a clear TelemetrySpoolError::InvalidMetadata for corrupt/oversized/invalid files. Added tests file_backed_spool_open_rejects_unsupported_schema_version and file_backed_spool_open_rejects_oversized_persisted_file.
results.notes: Validation runs only when an existing file is loaded; the freshly-initialized default state is constructed with the supported schema version.

results.summary: LineIndex::visible_line_slices now validates end_line_exclusive <= self.line_count() and returns TextError::LineOutOfBounds BEFORE the Vec::with_capacity, so a huge end_line_exclusive can no longer force a massive allocation ahead of the per-line bounds check. Added test visible_line_slices_reject_out_of_bounds_range_before_allocating.
results.notes: Fix is in the LineIndex method; TextSnapshot/TextBuffer visible_line_slices both forward to it.

results.summary: rebuild_from_simple_edit now returns None when the shifted edited chunk's byte_len exceeds DEFAULT_CHUNK_FORCE_MAX_BYTES, so try_replace_range falls back to refresh_cache_and_index(rebuild_chunk_index), restoring the bounded-chunk invariant instead of growing a chunk past 96 KiB on the simple-edit fast path. Added test simple_edit_growing_chunk_past_bound_falls_back_to_bounded_rebuild.

TextSnapshot::text() no longer panics: it returns the bounded cache when present, otherwise lazily materializes the full text from the rope once via a new OnceLock<Arc<String>> field. Added test snapshot_text_does_not_panic_for_degraded_cache. TextBuffer::text() still panics (documented) because it is the mutable editing surface where a materialized fallback would risk staleness across edits and silently reintroduce unbounded retention.
results.notes: Could not make TextBuffer::text() non-panicking without either changing the public -> &str signature (would ripple to dependent crates; the safe fallible try_full_text() already exists) or adding interior-mutability with invalidation on every mutation (staleness risk not worth it for a low finding). Snapshot path (the immutable read surface the recipe targets) is fully fixed.

results.summary: Added sanitize_terminal_text helper (escapes C0 controls except newline/tab, DEL 0x7f, and C1 controls 0x80-0x9f as visible \xNN) and routed every untrusted Display-rendered projection string in Shell::render through it: layout title, tab titles, close-dirty prompt, small-buffer text, viewport visible_text, file path, command id/scope, proposal lifecycle label/title, artifact id/state, verification run id/class, manifest id/item id/selected, inspector exposure id, budget id, assist provider/route/preview ids, prediction labels, workflow artifact ids/labels, hover label/summary, operation id, terminal denial/payload, and debug config/breakpoint/frame/variable/watch/console labels. {:?}-formatted fields left as-is (escape_debug already neutralizes control chars).
results.notes: Added unit test sanitize_terminal_text_escapes_control_and_ansi_sequences covering ESC/ANSI, C0, DEL, C1, and preservation of newline/tab and multibyte text.

results.summary: parse_pos viewport branch now computes absolute offset slice.byte_range.start + (byte_offset - current_offset), validates it lies within the slice (<= byte_range.end), and rejects offsets outside every visible slice with ShellCommandError::InvalidPosition instead of falling through to (0,0).
results.notes: Added tests parse_pos_viewport_returns_absolute_byte_offset (asserts Some(1024)/Some(1029)) and parse_pos_viewport_rejects_offset_outside_visible_slices.

results.summary: parse_pos small-buffer branch now validates byte_offset <= text.len() and text.is_char_boundary(byte_offset), and the viewport branch guards relative char boundary; both return new ShellCommandError::InvalidPosition rather than coercing to (0,0,0). parse_pos and command_position changed to Result<TextCoordinate, ShellCommandError> and propagated with ? at all call sites (:assist-predict, :hover, :completion, :definition, :references, :rename, :d, :r, :code-action). Non-numeric command_position payloads now rejected instead of coercing to start.
results.notes: Signature change is internal to legion-ui (parse_pos/command_position are private methods). Added tests parse_pos_rejects_out_of_bounds_offset, parse_pos_rejects_mid_codepoint_offset, parse_pos_accepts_in_bounds_char_boundary_offsets.

results.summary: Added .filter(|value| *value != 0) to parse_proposal_id between parse and map(ProposalId), mirroring parse_buffer_id, so :proposal-approve 0 (and other proposal commands) no longer emit a sentinel-id intent and fall through to Noop.
results.notes: Added test parse_proposal_id_rejects_zero_sentinel asserting 0/blank yield None, 42 yields Some, and :proposal-approve 0 yields Noop (no ApproveProposal intent).

results.summary: Threaded a per-message index discriminator from from_status_messages_with_verbosity (via .enumerate() before filtering) into from_status_message and folded it into toast_id, so two identical status messages now produce distinct toast ids; dismissing one no longer collapses duplicates.
results.notes: index enumerated over the original messages slice for stability across verbosity filtering. Added test toast_ids_distinguish_identical_status_messages. from_status_message is pub but only used within legion-ui (legion-desktop uses from_status_messages_with_verbosity), so the added parameter does not ripple.

results.summary: Added has_executable_entrypoint() that detects non-empty main/browser fields in package.json. manifest_from_package_json now threads this flag into required_tier (floors tier to Tier2ExtensionHostSidecar) and requested_capabilities (inserts vscode.extension.activate). An extension with executable main and only declarative contributions/no activation events now resolves to Tier2/SupportedWithPolicy and a NodeSidecar host session instead of Tier0/Supported/NoneRequired. aggregate_status already returns SupportedWithPolicy for non-Tier0, so no change needed there. Added two tests (executable_entrypoint_requires_host_policy_without_activation_events, blank_executable_entrypoint_stays_declarative).

required_string now maps to s.trim().to_string() before the non-empty filter so publisher/name/version identity fields are normalized before building VsCodeExtensionId, preventing malformed ids from whitespace. Added test identity_fields_are_trimmed_before_building_extension_id asserting padded inputs yield 'legion.trim-fixture'.

In ProviderRouter::route_completion, after obtaining `completion` I added a guard that returns a refused_response with reason_code `provider.identity_mismatch` when `completion.provider != request.provider_id || completion.model != request.model_label`, so a provider cannot silently substitute a different provider/model. Added unit test `router_refuses_when_provider_returns_mismatched_identity` with a `MismatchedIdentityProvider` mock.
results.notes: Kept the response echoing request provider/model on the success path (now guaranteed equal to completion's), matching existing API shape.

results.summary: Changed `allowed_route_decision()` to `allowed_route_decision(schema_version: u16)` and used the parameter for the nested AssistedAiRouteDecision.schema_version; call site now passes `request.schema_version`. Added unit test `route_decision_propagates_request_schema_version` asserting the nested decision's schema_version tracks the request (=2).

No telemetry code exists in package legion-ai. Grep for telemetry/TelemetrySpool/HostedTelemetry across crates/legion-ai/src returns nothing; package contains only lib.rs, redaction.rs, streaming.rs. The described Option<HostedTelemetrySpoolRecord> helper is not present here.
results.notes: If the helper exists at all it would live in a different package (e.g. legion-telemetry/legion-observability), which is out of scope for this agent.

results.summary: Added #[ignore = "performance suite: 100MB report-only measurement"] to large_file_100mb_degraded_mode_measurement so the 100 MiB allocation no longer runs on default `cargo test`. A smaller deterministic degraded-mode default test (ci_large_file_degraded_open_and_viewport_are_bounded) already exists.

The same 100MB test is now #[ignore], i.e. a dedicated report-only measurement excluded from the default pass/fail suite (the recipe's accepted resolution); its eprintln! latency report still runs under `cargo test -- --ignored`.

Added small deterministic non-ignored default-run companions: undo_redo_round_trips_content_for_default_runs (asserts undo/redo content round-trip) and snapshot_retention_and_release_for_default_runs (asserts pin counts before/after save+ack). Heavy benchmark variants remain #[ignore].

In apply_edits, deltas are now recomputed against the final staged buffer. Edits still apply descending (no offset drift during mutation), then a second ascending pass carries a cumulative length shift so each ChangedDelta byte_range/utf16_range reflects the higher edit's post-edit position instead of the original offset. Added regression test batch_edit_deltas_track_cumulative_shift_from_lower_edits.

set_cursors now validates every Cursor.position via buffer.try_byte_offset before storing; set_selections validates both range endpoints and start<=end (returning EditorError::InvalidEdit on inversion). Invalid editor state can no longer be persisted, so viewport_projection's protocol_range/try_byte_offset calls cannot fail on stored state.

EditorRequest::ApplyTransaction no longer returns a false EditorResponse::Transaction success. A TextTransactionDescriptor carries only changed-range metadata (no replacement text), so mutation is impossible from it; the handler now validates buffer identity then returns ProtocolError::unsupported directing callers to ApplyEdit (fail-closed). Identity-mismatch error path preserved.

EditorRequest::Overlay no longer returns a false OverlayApplied success. The DiagnosticOverlay request carries no buffer/workspace identity to resolve a target, and engine.set_overlays results are never projected anywhere (state.overlays is write-only). Handler now returns ProtocolError::unsupported (fail-closed) until overlay routing is implemented.

Resolved the contract contradiction by aligning the viewport message with the implemented-and-tested behavior. request_save already assembles degraded-file text from chunks, and large_file_save_assembles_from_chunks + large_file_undo_redo_round_trips_content assert this success path. The misleading LargeFileStatus message claiming 'Phase 1 saves fail closed' was updated to state saves assemble the full file from chunks on request.
results.notes: Chose the 'support degraded saves' contract over fail-closed because existing passing tests enforce that degraded saves succeed via chunk assembly; failing closed would have regressed working, tested functionality. EditorError::DegradedSaveUnavailable remains a public (unused) variant — no new warning since it is a pub enum variant. Separately fixed a pre-existing/parallel build break: legion_protocol::ViewportProjection gained a required `cursors: Vec<TextCoordinate>` field; the editor constructor now populates it from buffer cursor state (lib.rs ~1046).

results.summary: Split bundled-Rust support from plugin registration. Added private helper tree_sitter_language_is_bundled_rust (rust/rs only). highlight_captures_from_text and code_chunk_descriptors_from_text now gate on the bundled-Rust check (return empty for plugin/unsupported languages instead of running the Rust query). TreeSitterParser::parse now only runs the Rust tree-sitter path for rust/rs; plugin-registered languages route to LexicalFallbackParser and push an 'index.tree_sitter.plugin_grammar_unsupported' Warning diagnostic to both file_index.diagnostics and outcome.diagnostics. tree_sitter_supports_language still reports plugin grammars as supported but they no longer parse as Rust.
results.notes: Updated the existing test (which codified the buggy as-Rust behavior) to assert lexical fallback + diagnostic, and added bundled_rust_still_parses_via_tree_sitter to lock in the positive path. Real loaded-grammar worker for plugin grammars remains future work; fail-soft fallback is in place.

results.summary: Replaced all three .lock().expect(...) calls on PLUGIN_TREE_SITTER_GRAMMAR_REGISTRY (register_plugin_tree_sitter_grammars, plugin_tree_sitter_grammar_is_registered, reset_plugin_tree_sitter_grammar_registry_for_tests) with .unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner) so a poisoned lock recovers instead of panicking the indexing process. Added PoisonError to the std::sync import.

In build_rename_preview_payload, changed coverage_kind from Complete to Partial because a single SymbolFileMapRecord only carries this file's shallow declaration/reference ranges and cannot prove cross-file references were searched. Also replaced the empty diagnostics vec with an 'index.rename.single_file_coverage' Warning explaining cross-file references were not searched.
results.notes: SymbolFileMapRecord carries no workspace-complete/freshness proof (reference_ranges are documented as 'known from the shallow map'), so Partial is always correct here. Updated the workflow test that asserted Complete.

results.summary: read_until_correlated_response no longer drops responses carrying a different json_rpc_id. Added a response_stash: HashMap<u64, LspCorrelatedResponse> to LspStdioSession; out-of-order responses are now correlated and stashed by json_rpc_id, and read_response_for drains the stash before reading new frames. Unknown/already-resolved ids (e.g. late responses for cancelled requests) are still skipped.
results.notes: Existing session contract tests (cancel/late-response, multi-request reuse) still pass.

results.summary: Added a deadline derived from pending.timeout_ms around the read loop in read_until_correlated_response (Instant-based, checked between frames). On expiry it calls self.client.resolve_timeout(expected_request_id, elapsed_ms) and returns the Timeout-status LspCorrelatedResponse instead of blocking until EOF. read_response_for now threads pending.timeout_ms through.
results.notes: Bounds the wait whenever the server keeps emitting frames (notifications / other responses). A fully SILENT server is still bounded only by the future async read pump the type docs describe: std pipe reads have no portable timeout, so the deadline can only be observed between frames without a dedicated reader thread. The type is documented as intentionally synchronous (WS03.T1), so the async pump/$/cancelRequest emission remains as the follow-up; no insecure silent-proceed path was left in place.

results.summary: prepare_request now uses checked_add(1) and returns LspRuntimeResult<LspPendingRequest>, surfacing a new LspRuntimeError::JsonRpcIdExhausted instead of saturating at u64::MAX and reusing the id (which corrupted the correlation tables). send_request propagates with `?`; the 5 in-package test call sites in lifecycle_contract.rs were updated with .unwrap().
results.notes: Signature change is confined to this package (only callers are send_request and in-crate tests; legion-protocol has an unrelated prepare_request of its own).

results.summary: completion_projection_for_item now computes score_basis_points in u32: penalty = (index as u32).saturating_mul(100); 10_000u32.saturating_sub(penalty) as u16. The result is always <= 10_000 so the final u16 cast is lossless, and large indices can no longer wrap to a top score.

mock_lsp_server read_frame now rejects Content-Length greater than a new MAX_FRAME_PAYLOAD_BYTES (64 MiB, mirroring LspFramer::MAX_FRAME_PAYLOAD_BYTES) with MockIoError::Protocol before allocating the payload buffer, matching the production read_lsp_frame bound.

The unknown-method (`other`) arm now uses id.map(...) so unknown NOTIFICATIONS (id absent) get no response, while unknown REQUESTS still receive the -32601 error. This stops the mock emitting `"id": null` responses to notifications.

rust_analyzer_initializes_against_legion_repo_when_opted_in is now an #[ignore]-gated live-smoke target. The two silent early returns were converted to hard failures: it asserts LEGION_RUN_RUST_ANALYZER_SMOKE=1 and asserts the rust-analyzer binary is available (panic instead of eprintln+return), so the real-server launch path cannot silently rot. Run via `cargo test -- --ignored` in a dedicated CI job.
results.notes: Verified it now reports as `ignored` in the default test run.

results.summary: Replaced the unused underscore bindings in the rust-analyzer smoke with real assertions on the deterministic surfaces: completion contains an `Alpha` entry; hover label is non-empty and mentions `add`; definition/declaration/references resolve at least one location; signatureHelp has a non-empty `signatures` array; document outline and workspace symbols include `add`; folding ranges and semantic-token `data` are non-empty. Per the fix recipe's narrowing option, implementation/typeDefinition/inlayHint/codeLens remain smoke-exercised only (legitimately empty/config-dependent for a free function) with explanatory comments rather than flaky assertions.
results.notes: Assertions run only under the #[ignore] live job; could not execute them here (no rust-analyzer in this environment), but they compile under clippy --all-targets.

results.summary: Added protocol_workspace_configs/protocol_file_metadata/protocol_sessions (#[serde(default)] maps) and protocol_trust (#[serde(default)] Vec, because its (WorkspaceId,PrincipalId) tuple key cannot be a JSON map key) to PersistedState; copied them in From<&InMemoryStorage>; restored them in From<PersistedState> (rebuilding the trust map from the record list) instead of HashMap::new(). Added flush/reopen regression test file_backed_storage_roundtrips_protocol_workspace_metadata_session_and_trust exercising SaveWorkspaceConfig/SaveFileMetadata/SaveSessionRecord/SaveTrustRecord.
results.notes: protocol_trust persisted as a flat Vec<TrustRecord> and rebuilt on load because serde_json rejects tuple map keys; all other three persist as native maps with integer/string keys.

results.summary: Reordered record_event to persist+validate event metadata first (self.handle(SaveEventMetadata)? ) and only then emit. If emit fails after a successful store it now returns an explicit partial-failure ProtocolError (code storage_event_emit_failed_after_store) while keeping the persisted record, instead of emitting before storing. Added test record_event_persists_before_emitting_and_fails_closed_on_store_error verifying no event is emitted when the store fails and exactly one is emitted on success.

Replaced the integrity verification with a SHA-256 digest (new storage_checksum + inline pure-Rust sha256_digest, no new crate dependency since sha2 is not a dep of legion-storage and Cargo.toml must not be edited). STORAGE_CHECKSUM_ALGORITHM is now legion-storage-sha256-v1; backup_file uses storage_checksum; recover_from_backup verifies sha256 for new markers and keeps a legacy branch (LEGACY_STORAGE_CHECKSUM_ALGORITHM via stable_storage_sum) for old sum:len markers, returning algorithm-mismatch otherwise. Added test storage_checksum_is_collision_resistant_and_order_sensitive (known SHA-256 vectors + permutation that fools the legacy sum now differs).
results.notes: Implemented SHA-256 inline rather than via the sha2 crate because adding a dependency requires editing Cargo.toml, which is out of scope; legacy checksum retained read-only for backward compatibility.

results.summary: In FileBackedStorage::open the corrupt-parse branch now captures fs::rename's Result: on Ok it returns StorageError::Corrupt with the quarantine_path (move actually happened); on Err it returns StorageError::Failed describing the quarantine failure, so a failed rename no longer reports the file as quarantined while leaving the corrupt primary in place.
results.notes: No new unit test: simulating a rename failure portably (held handle / read-only dir) is platform-dependent; existing file_backed_storage_corrupt_file_is_quarantined covers the success path.

results.summary: crates/legion-storage/src/secrets.rs does not exist; InMemorySecretStore/SecretStoreError appear nowhere in this package (only lib.rs is present). The described behavior is not implemented here.
results.notes: No secret-store implementation exists in legion-storage.

results.summary: provider_secret_reference and the format!("{provider_id}:{secret_name}") keyring-account construction do not exist anywhere in legion-storage (no secrets.rs).

The string-matching keyring 'not found' classification does not exist in legion-storage. The only keyring usage in the repo is legion-retention's keyring_error, which lives in a different package and must not be edited from here.
results.notes: Real location is legion-retention (keyring_error), outside this package's scope.

results.summary: Replaced the inert placeholder_docs_hygiene_test_file_compiles test with docs_hygiene_passes_for_clean_minimal_repo, which actually calls run_docs_hygiene on a clean fixture and asserts it passes.
results.notes: Distinct from the release_pipeline 'Finding 17' (xtask.md).

results.summary: docs_hygiene_checks_untracked_markdown_in_git_repo now captures the `git init` and `git add` outputs and asserts output.status.success(), printing stdout/stderr on failure, so a failed git fixture no longer silently degrades the test.

Extracted a pure classify_skeleton_status(total, budget) in perf_harness.rs (now the single source of truth used by plan_perf_harness) and replaced the two stub tests with perf_harness_classifies_status_failed_when_total_exceeds_budget (deterministically asserts Failed/Passed/Skipped) plus a zero-budget Skipped test.

Added pub apply_fail_on_budget_value(&mut, &str) that takes an injected value; apply_fail_on_budget_override now delegates to it. The test was rewritten to call apply_fail_on_budget_value with literal strings, removing all process-global env mutation (no more unsafe set_var/remove_var race).

release_pipeline TempRepo::new now includes std::process::id() and a static AtomicU64 counter in the temp dir name, matching the docs_hygiene/perf fixtures, guaranteeing uniqueness under coarse clock granularity.

Added a `#[arg(long = "no-strict")] no_strict: bool` negation flag to all four subcommands (PerfHarness, VerifyPerfHarness, LegionBench, VerifyLegionBench) and resolve effective strict as `strict && !no_strict` in the main dispatch, so --no-strict now actually disables strict mode as the docs claim.
results.notes: Existing default-true behavior preserved; --no-strict now works as documented.

results.summary: Added a `--config` arg to the VerifyReleasePipeline variant (matching ReleasePipeline) and threaded it into run_verify_release_pipeline_command, so a custom config used for release-pipeline can be verified instead of always loading the default example config.

Rewrote markdown_section to iterate lines via a new parse_atx_heading helper: it now requires a real ATX heading line whose level and label match (no more substring matches in prose/code blocks) and terminates at the next heading whose level <= the matched level. Added three unit tests.

check_markdown_links now resolves normal relative links only against file.parent(); dropped the root.join(&normalized) fallback so a nested broken link (e.g. README.md) no longer passes just because the repo root has a same-named file. Leading-`/` root-relative handling is unchanged.

run_docs_hygiene no longer silently `continue`s on read error; it pushes a new DocsHygieneViolationKind::UnreadableFile violation so an unreadable tracked markdown file causes a non-zero exit (fail-closed).

is_allowlisted now matches on path-segment boundaries via a new path_has_prefix helper (rel == prefix or rel starts with prefix+'/'), so allowlist `docs/foo` no longer matches sibling `docs/foo-old.md`.

Changed BacklogCard.dependencies to Option<Vec<String>> (removed #[serde(default)]) so an omitted field deserializes to None vs. `[]` being Some([]); check_required_fields now requires dependencies.is_some(), and validation iterates dependencies.iter().flatten(). Updated existing literals and added two tests (omitted rejected, present-empty accepted). Verified the real backlog declares dependencies for all 146 tasks.

verify_legion_bench_report now does full task equality (expected != &result.task) in the zip loop and recomputes the summary from report.tasks via a shared recompute_summary helper (also used by plan_legion_bench_report), rejecting any mismatch. This catches tampered task fields, tampered scores (via average_score), and tampered summary counts. Added two tamper-rejection tests.

score_task no longer conflates tests_passed with require_tests_pass: it now sets tests_passed=true (synthetic baseline run passes) and gates with tests_gate = !require_tests_pass || tests_passed, so a task with require_tests_pass=false can pass. Default-suite behavior (all require_tests_pass=true) is unchanged.
run_no_egui_textedit no longer `continue`s on read error; it pushes a new NoEguiTextEditViolationKind::UnreadableFile violation so an unreadable scanned file causes a non-zero exit (fail-closed).
is_scanned and is_allowlisted now match on path-segment boundaries via a new path_has_prefix helper, so a configured prefix no longer matches sibling paths that merely share the byte prefix.

plan_release_pipeline now passes the channel-adjusted `&version` (not `&workspace_version`) into build_version_stamp, so version_stamp.package_version matches each descriptor's version (e.g. `0.1.0-preview` for the preview channel).

write_descriptors now pre-collects descriptor_file_stem values into a HashSet and returns an explicit collision error before writing anything, so two installer-target names normalizing to the same `<stem>.toml` no longer silently overwrite. Added a collision test.
results.notes: Distinct from the docs_hygiene-test 'Finding 17' (tests---remaining-crates.md).

results.summary: Added shared_blocking_client() (OnceLock) built via reqwest::blocking::Client::builder().connect_timeout(10s).timeout(120s) and reused it at all four blocking HTTP sites (ReqwestProviderHttpTransport::post_json, Anthropic post_json/post_text, StreamableHttpMcpTransport::send). Timeout/connect failures map to ProviderError::RequestFailed / McpClientError::Transport as before. Also fixed a latent panic: the provider HTTP path built a rustls client without installing a crypto provider; shared_blocking_client now installs it eagerly. Added test shared_blocking_client_builds_with_timeouts.
results.notes: A true network-hang timeout test would require a deliberately slow server; the added test verifies the timeout-configured client builds. Real elapsed-timeout behavior is enforced by reqwest's connect_timeout/timeout.

results.summary: OpenAiCompatibleProvider::complete now builds the chat/completions payload incrementally and only sets max_tokens/temperature when Some(..), mirroring OpenAiResponsesProvider::request_payload, so unset options are omitted instead of serialized as JSON null. Added test openai_compatible_provider_omits_unset_sampling_options.

Introduced AnthropicCredentialKind (ApiKey|AuthToken) and AnthropicCredential; transport post_json/post_text now send x-api-key for API keys and Authorization: Bearer only for auth tokens via apply_anthropic_credential. from_env detects the kind per matched env var (preserving precedence); added credential_kind field, with_transport_kind constructor, and credential() helper. Updated the AnthropicMessagesTransport trait signature (package-internal; no external impls). Added test anthropic_api_key_uses_x_api_key_and_auth_token_uses_bearer.
results.notes: Trait signature change is confined to this package (grep found no external implementors/callers). Existing with_transport keeps its public signature and defaults to ApiKey kind.

results.summary: Changed OpenAiResponsesProvider::request_payload store default from true to false so provider-side response storage requires explicit openai.responses.store=true opt-in. Added test openai_responses_store_defaults_to_false_without_metadata.

Reworked StdioMcpSession to read stdout on a dedicated thread that pushes lines over an mpsc channel; send_on_session now enforces a per-request deadline (STDIO_MCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT=30s) via recv_timeout, returning McpClientError::Transport on timeout, channel error, or disconnect. This bounds the time the session mutex is held and prevents a wedged/silent or notification-flooding server from hanging the caller indefinitely. Drop joins the reader thread after killing the child (pipe closes -> EOF).
results.notes: Full fix: the reader-thread + recv_timeout design gives a real wall-clock deadline even for a silent server, not just a flood bound.

results.summary: Hardened spawn_http_fixture: listener set non-blocking with an accept loop bounded by HTTP_FIXTURE_DEADLINE (20s), per-stream read/write timeouts (10s), and a while handled<4 loop that self-terminates at the deadline. Because the fixture thread now always exits, the test's handle.join() is bounded; an early client failure surfaces as the existing requests.len()==4 assertion instead of an indefinite hang.

Replaced the hard-coded python3 stdio fixtures with a Cargo-built Rust fixture binary at src/bin/mcp_stdio_fixture.rs (auto-discovered, so no Cargo.toml edit), referenced from tests via env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_mcp_stdio_fixture"). The binary reimplements both the conformance and pid JSON-RPC fixture behaviors (mode arg). Removed the python script helpers; stdio_transport/stdio_pid_transport now invoke the binary. External python3 runtime dependency eliminated.
results.notes: New binary is a normal package target using serde_json (already a non-dev dependency); builds and passes clippy under --all-targets -D warnings.

results.summary: Added MemoryError::UnsupportedSnapshotVersion variant and a SUPPORTED_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION=1 constant; from_snapshot now rejects schema_version 0 and any version above the supported max before constructing Self. Added test from_snapshot_rejects_unsupported_schema_versions.

Added an explicit candidate_id.trim().is_empty() guard at the start of validate_memory_candidate returning InvalidProposalMetadata, mirroring validate_legion_workflow_candidate. Added test memory_candidate_rejects_empty_candidate_id.

Converted retain, retain_legion_workflow_candidate, and retain_trace_record to upsert: replace an existing entry with the same candidate_id/trace_id instead of blindly pushing, so duplicates can no longer accumulate. Delete semantics (filter-by-id) remain consistent with the now-unique keys. Added test repeated_retain_does_not_duplicate_records covering all three paths.

Replaced the hard-coded EventSequence(13) in from_session_metadata with EventSequence(session.generated_at.0), deriving a distinct sequence from session metadata. Kept the public signature unchanged (avoiding ripple to legion-app which calls this), per the internal-fix preference. Added test workflow_candidate_sequence_is_derived_from_session_metadata.
results.notes: from_session_metadata is also called from crates/legion-app/src/lib.rs, so the signature was preserved and the sequence is derived internally instead of adding a parameter.

results.summary: dispatch_host_call now computes next_output_bytes = output_bytes_used.saturating_add(label.len()) and denies with QuotaExceeded when the cumulative total exceeds max_output_bytes; only commits the accumulated value on accept. Added test plugin_runtime_accumulates_output_bytes_across_calls.

Added explicit begin_invocation/end_invocation methods on PluginRuntimeHost; begin_invocation resets host_calls_used and output_bytes_used to 0 (sets Running), end_invocation returns to Idle, making the per-invocation quota docs accurate. Added test plugin_runtime_resets_quota_counters_on_invocation_boundary.

CommandDescriptor and Contribution arms of PluginRuntimePort::handle now verify the item against loaded state. register_command requires a loaded/activated plugin that declares the required capability AND has a matching Command contribution in its manifest, else returns ProtocolError plugin_command_not_registered. register_contribution requires the descriptor's plugin_id to resolve to a loaded/activated plugin, else plugin_not_loaded/plugin_not_active. Added two tests.
results.notes: PluginCommandDescriptor carries no PluginId (in legion-protocol, out of scope to change), so the command arm matches across loaded plugins by command_id + required_capability instead of a direct plugin lookup. ContributionDescriptor has no required_capability/contribution-name mapping into the manifest's heterogeneous contribution enum, so the contribution arm enforces the plugin-loaded/activated check (the reliably verifiable part) rather than a fragile name match that could reject valid contributions.

results.summary: Replaced the DefaultHasher body of stable_hash with a fixed 128-bit FNV-1a implementation (full-width, deterministic across compiler/std versions) and mixed in a STABLE_HASH_VERSION tag so persisted/protocol-facing ids (WorkspaceId, git hunk ids, content-version digest) are namespaced by algorithm version. Added a unit test asserting determinism, no-collision, and 128-bit width.
results.notes: All three consumers (WorkspaceId@~455, hunk id@~1790 via {:032x}, content digest@~3447 via `as u64`) now benefit from a stable algorithm with no API change.

results.summary: Removed the gix Debug-format scraping (git_status_entries_gix now delegates to the authoritative CLI porcelain parser, mirroring git_blame_lines_gix) and deleted the fragile extract_gix_status_path/extract_gix_status_code helpers. Extracted the porcelain parsing into a pure parse_git_porcelain_status fn and added a rename/copy/delete/untracked/modified unit test.
results.notes: Preserved the existing CLI parser's rename/copy keying behavior exactly (keys on trailing source field) to avoid introducing new behavior. The gix dependency is now unused in source but Cargo.toml is out of scope; no unused-dependency lint is enabled so build/clippy stay clean.

results.summary: WorkspaceSearchHit::line_number is user-facing; changed both search sites to (line_number as u32).saturating_add(1) so the first line reports 1, matching the one-based blame convention, and documented the field as one-based.
results.notes: No existing tests asserted specific search-hit line numbers.

results.summary: Cited file crates/legion-ai-providers/tests/smoke.rs does not exist and the described hosted-Anthropic smoke test is in a different package (legion-ai-providers), outside legion-project scope.
results.notes: Real location would be legion-ai-providers if reintroduced; not editable here.

results.summary: Cited file crates/legion-plugin/tests/hostile.rs does not exist; legion-plugin has no tests/ dir. This is a different package, outside legion-project scope.
results.notes: Real location would be legion-plugin if reintroduced; not editable here.

results.summary: Cited file crates/legion-plugin/tests/quotas.rs does not exist; legion-plugin has no tests/ dir. Different package, outside legion-project scope.
results.notes: Real location would be legion-plugin if reintroduced; not editable here.

results.summary: Replaced the fixed 120ms sleep in indexed_workspace_search_refreshes_after_file_changes with a bounded retry loop (5s deadline, 20ms backoff) that drains poll_watcher_events and re-runs the indexed search until the invalidation is observed (hit_count==0).
results.notes: Test verified passing.

results.summary: Added a non-ignored test indexed_workspace_search_matches_live_scan_for_dozens_of_files (32 files) validating indexed-vs-live equivalence for larger fixtures, complementing the still-#[ignore]d 500-file benchmark so default runs cover multi-file indexed search.
results.notes: Test verified passing.

results.summary: Replaced the fixed 80ms sleep in watcher_recovery_overflow_then_rescan_emits_recovery_event with a bounded poll loop (5s deadline, 20ms backoff) retrying poll_watcher_events until the Modified recovery event appears.
results.notes: Recovery is NOT synchronous (gated behind the 64ms WATCHER_RENAME_DEBOUNCE_MILLIS debounce), so a plain sleep removal failed; the bounded poll loop is the correct robust fix per the recipe. Verified passing.

results.summary: Introduced an RAII TempWorkspace struct (Deref<Target=Path>, Drop removes the root via remove_dir_all and any tracked outside fixtures via remove_file). create_temp_workspace now returns it; removed all manual `let _ = remove_dir_all(root);` cleanup lines and the symlink test's outside-file cleanup, which is now tracked via track_outside.
results.notes: Deref coercion keeps all existing &root/root.join call sites unchanged. track_outside is cfg-allowed dead on non-unix (only the unix symlink test uses it). All path_boundary tests verified passing.

results.summary: Added `|| self.failed_verification_count > 0` to the Ready-state validation guard in LegionWorkflowTrackerRecord::validate so a merge-ready record with failed/blocked verification gates is now rejected. Added regression test legion_workflow_tracker_rejects_failed_verification_ready_state.
results.notes: Defect re-confirmed at the renamed/current location (validate fn, Ready guard). failed_verification_count was previously never validated.

results.summary: initialize() now passes sandbox_path to `git worktree add` via .arg(&self.sandbox_path) (OsStr) instead of .to_str().unwrap(), removing the non-UTF-8 path panic.

validate_containment now returns the normalized sandbox-relative PathBuf and rejects residual ParentDir/RootDir/Prefix components; generate_proposal builds the CanonicalPath from that normalized path (joined with '/'), so `..` segments no longer persist in the emitted payload.
results.notes: validate_containment signature changed from Result<(),_> to Result<PathBuf,_>; it is only used within this crate (one call site + tests), so no cross-crate ripple.

results.summary: generate_proposal now reads the target's on-disk state (new base_target_preconditions helper) and populates expected_file_length/expected_modified_at/expected_fingerprint as a concurrency guard; misleading 'compares with HEAD' doc corrected. Added a test asserting preconditions populate when the target exists and stay None for a genuine create.
results.notes: Payload still ProposalPayload::CreateFile even when a base file exists, because emitting a TextEditProposal requires a FileId/EditBatch that the generator's inputs do not provide. The security-relevant defect (no concurrency guard) is resolved; the create-vs-edit payload distinction remains. Fingerprint uses std DefaultHasher (algorithm label 'std-default-hasher-v1') since no crypto-hash crate is available in this package's deps.

results.summary: record_proposal_output now fails closed with AgentError::InvalidLegionWorkflow when an evidence_id/result_id for the worker already exists, preventing colliding ids and duplicate worker_results/evidence_records. Added a duplicate-record test.

provider_route_request_from_worker now derives cancellation_token and event_sequence per-worker via new derive_cancellation_token/derive_event_sequence helpers (deterministic DefaultHasher over worker_id + correlation_id) instead of the constant 13. Added a test asserting two provider workers get distinct audit identities.
results.notes: Identities are deterministic per (worker_id, correlation_id) rather than a true monotonic session-supplied sequence; a session-threaded monotonic counter would require a signature/state change beyond this static helper. The core defect (all routes sharing one cancellation/audit identity) is resolved.

results.summary: main() now tracks whether a path arg was explicitly supplied. When the scratch.txt default is used and the file does not exist, it opens via open_new_file (CreateNew intent) instead of open_file (Existing intent), so startup no longer aborts on a missing scratch file. Explicit user-supplied paths still use open_file.

Added `prompt_prefix: String::new()` to the AssistedAiProviderRouteRequest literal in the #[cfg(test)] route_request() helper. Verified with `cargo build -p legion-app --no-default-features --all-targets` (offline_ai compiled) which now succeeds, and the offline_ai tests pass.

validate_containment no longer panics: both std::env::current_dir().unwrap() calls now propagate via a closure returning OfflineAiError::InvalidMetadata (fn already returns Result). Added canonicalize_existing_prefix() which canonicalizes the longest existing ancestor of the candidate (resolving symlinks in the on-disk portion) and re-appends the non-existent tail, then compares canonical paths so a symlink placed inside the sandbox can no longer escape via lexical-only normalization.

Confirmed proposal.rs does not exist in legion-app/src and no accepted-target/filtered-batch code (filtered_batch, for_accepted, build_filtered, accepted_targets, filter_proposal) matches anywhere in the package. The described defect has no location in the current tree.
results.notes: Planned split-module layout never landed; the described filtered-batch edge-dropping behavior does not exist in this package.

results.summary: Eliminated the disk-vs-buffer inconsistency on post-commit audit failure. SaveWorkflowFailure gained a `committed: Option<SaveWorkflowOutput>` field; only the Ok(applied) branch's observe_proposal_response failure sets it (the on-disk write already committed). save_buffer's Err arm now, when `committed` is present, acknowledges the save and calls bind_saved_buffer + clear_dirty_prompt_for to reconcile editor state with the persisted bytes before surfacing the audit failure as Rejected, so disk and editor state stay consistent. All other failure paths set committed: None (no disk mutation).
results.notes: Implemented recipe option 3 (commit saved metadata while surfacing the audit failure) rather than a pre-save rollback, because SaveWorkflowService is a free function without the &mut self checkpoint machinery; this fully resolves the dirty-buffer-over-saved-disk inconsistency.

results.summary: The post-initialize ACP block's two early returns now run orchestrator.cleanup(&permission) before returning. acp_host_command.run(...) and std::fs::read_to_string(...) were converted from `?` to match arms that, on Err, run cleanup and surface cleanup failures alongside the original error message before returning AppCompositionError::AiRuntime. The sandbox is no longer leaked on these failure paths.

Confirmed no filtered/partial delegated-apply path exists: filtered_batch, build_filtered, filter_proposal, filtered_apply_required, accepted_targets match nowhere in legion-app/src. The described projection-vs-apply divergence cannot be located.
results.notes: No subset/partial-apply code in this package; described behavior does not exist.

results.summary: parse_terminal_shell_output now tracks whether the OSC sequence was terminated (BEL/ST). On an unterminated sequence it pushes the raw bytes from the ESC] introducer onward into visible_output and breaks, instead of silently dropping the trailing remainder. Added unit tests parse_terminal_keeps_unterminated_osc_bytes and parse_terminal_handles_terminated_osc.

terminal_shell_cwd_from_osc now URL-aware: splits host from path (file:/// => empty host, localhost treated as empty), percent-decodes each path segment via a new percent_decode helper, special-cases Windows drive-letter paths (file:///C:/Users -> C:/Users via is_windows_drive_prefix) and UNC paths (file://server/share -> //server/share). Added tests osc7_cwd_decodes_windows_drive_and_percent and osc7_cwd_handles_localhost_and_unc.

Replaced the per-token comment predicate with a proper overlap rejection over the full delimiter span. Rust: reject Comment tokens where start_col < rust_slashes+2 && end_col > rust_slashes; TOML: reject where start_col <= toml_hash && end_col > toml_hash. A comment token starting at the second slash inside a string is now correctly flagged as a failure.

Introduced a Drop-guarded TempWorkspace (Deref+AsRef<Path>) returned by create_root(); Drop does a prefix/location-checked remove_dir_all (temp_dir + 'legion-app-daily-editing-'). Removed all 11 manual `let _ = remove_dir_all(&root)` calls so panics no longer leak temp roots.

Bound the git init Output and assert status.success(), including status code, stdout and stderr in the failure message, so a failed `git init` no longer lets the gitignore test continue in a non-repo.

Made temp_workspace return a Drop-guarded TempWorkspace (Deref+AsRef<Path>) with a prefix/location-checked remove_dir_all on drop ('legion_app_delegated_'); removed the three ad-hoc remove_dir_all calls so all tests (waiting-plan, approved-plan, acp-host, hunk, chat) clean up uniformly even on panic.

Added a once-checked git_available() helper (OnceLock running `git --version`) and a fail-fast assert at the top of TempGitRepo::new() with an explicit environment diagnostic, so a missing git binary surfaces as a clear environment error rather than a confusing product-test failure inside run_git.

Added merge_feature_expecting_conflict() which captures the merge Output, asserts !status.success() and that `git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U` is non-empty (with stdout/stderr in messages), then replaced all five discarded-ExitStatus `git merge feature` blocks with this helper.

Replaced the single TerminalInput+OutputPoll+Search sequence with: send input, then a bounded poll loop (10s deadline, 25ms backoff) re-dispatching TerminalOutputPoll until output_rows is non-empty, then search; final assert reports the projection on failure. Removes the one-poll race on slow hosts with the real PTY fixture.

Re-rooted temp_workspace in std::env::temp_dir() (was current_dir/target) with a pid+timestamp+counter unique suffix and returned a Drop-guarded TempWorkspace (Deref+AsRef<Path>) doing a prefix/location-checked remove_dir_all ('legion-workflow-integration-'); removed the three manual remove_dir_all calls.

Removed the hard-coded /13.0 denominator (now cases.len()) and added a catalog-drift guard: enumerate the live registered command catalog via an OpenPalette '>' projection, assert every case title exists in the catalog (no stale cases), and assert catalog == case-titles UNION an explicit git-mutation allowlist so any new command added without a case/allowlist entry fails with a catalog-vs-cases diff. Also changed the loop to borrow `&cases` so the table is reusable after the loop.

Renamed app_plugin_manifest_registers_tree_sitter_grammar_through_phase_five_channel to ..._metadata_through_phase_five_channel and added a doc comment making explicit that it validates metadata registration only and deliberately does not create/validate/load the .wasm artifact (artifact_uri is a placeholder). Chose the recipe's second option because the loader does not enforce artifact existence and that enforcement lives in src/ (out of scope for tests/).
results.notes: Artifact existence/loadability is still not validated; enforcing it requires a loader change in crates/legion-app/src (and a real fixture artifact), which is outside the tests/ scope restriction.

results.summary: Extracted poll_terminal_until(app, session_id, condition) with a generous 10s deadline that re-dispatches TerminalOutputPoll until the exact condition holds and panics with the last projection on timeout; replaced the fixed `for _ in 0..20 { sleep 25ms }` loop (~500ms ceiling) with it.

Wrapped create_root() output in a Drop-guarded TempWorkspace (Deref+AsRef<Path>) whose Drop performs a prefix-checked remove_dir_all using 'legion-terminal-workflow-{pid}-'; removed the manual `remove_dir_all(&root).ok()` cleanup calls in all three tests.

Added a Drop-guarded OutsideFixture that builds the off-workspace path in root.parent() with a fresh Uuid::now_v7() suffix (eliminating the colliding TEMP_ROOT_COUNTER.load name with no pid/timestamp) and removes the file on drop with a prefix/location check. Used it for both the legion-escape and legion-outside fixtures; for the traversal test the unique file_name is reused to build the ../../ path so outside_normalized still matches. Removed the manual remove_file calls.

Same fix as the app-&-agent Finding 12 (the cited legion-desktop palette_coverage.rs does not exist; the coverage test is in legion-app/tests/palette.rs). Removed the hard-coded /13.0 denominator (now cases.len()) and added a registered-command-catalog drift guard derived from the live palette projection.
results.notes: Finding's source_file path (legion-desktop) is wrong; the real test lives in crates/legion-app/tests/palette.rs. Resolved by the same edit as the app-&-agent Finding 12.

results.summary: platform.rs high_dpi_status now reports any positive finite scale (including exactly 1.0) as os-observed; NOT_OBSERVED reserved for None/non-finite/<=0.

temporary_session_path now appends a static AtomicU64 nonce alongside process::id(), so concurrent same-path saves get unique temp files; the pre-write remove_file is now scoped to that unique path. Validated by session_store_save_publishes_validated_temp_and_cleans_intermediates (passes).

Whole-document substring scan in reject_raw_source_markers is a genuine false-positive risk, but tests/session_restore.rs:338 (session_restore_store_rejects_raw_source_markers) explicitly asserts that a session_id of 'source_body' is rejected, enshrining the whole-document scan as intended defense-in-depth. Narrowing to field-scoped checks would break that test, which is out of scope (tests/). Left behavior unchanged.
results.notes: Blocked by out-of-scope test that depends on the current behavior; fix requires editing tests/session_restore.rs.

results.summary: Added DesktopBridgeError::MissingRemoteDefaultBranch; OpenGitPullRequestUrl now returns that typed error when remote_default_branch is None/empty/whitespace instead of falling back to the current branch (which produced branch...branch self-compare URLs).
OpenWorkspace Ok branch now assigns self.workspace_root = root.clone() and clears explorer_expansion so workspace-scoped state rebases to the newly opened root.

Added DesktopRuntime::dispatch_ui_action which captures the handle_action Result, sets an Error status and refreshes the projection on failure. Replaced all four discard sites (handle_keyboard loop, palette query change, palette row click delta+dispatch, eframe ui output loop).

editor_text_input_actions now tracks a running insertion coordinate: `at` is mutable and advanced via new advance_text_coordinate helper after each Text/Paste/ImeCommit action, so multiple text events in one frame target sequential positions instead of the original cursor offset.

Windows URL opener no longer goes through `cmd /C start`; new open_url_windows calls ShellExecuteW (linked from shell32, mirroring the existing MoveFileExW pattern) passing the URL as the single lpFile, so `&` and other metacharacters are not reinterpreted by cmd. Command import gated to non-windows.

Added normalize_inline (control chars -> space) and normalize_markdown (control chars -> space plus backslash-escaping of markdown-sensitive chars) and applied them to workspace_label and each unsupported surface in rows() and to_markdown(). Verified operational_health/diagnostics tests use .contains() substrings that still match.

FrameTimingRecorder now bounds both input_paint_samples and frame_durations_ms to MAX_RETAINED_SAMPLES (4096), evicting the oldest entry on push so a long-running session cannot grow them or summary()'s work unbounded.
Search result rows now pass row.snippet through a normalize_snippet helper (control chars -> spaces) before interpolation, preserving the one-line path:line:col snippet format.

WindowsPackageConfig gained an optional target_triple (plus with_target_triple builder; new() defaults to None preserving the 3-arg API). plan_windows_package appends --target <triple> to cargo_args and builds executable_source as target/<triple>/<profile>/legion-desktop.exe when set, and fails closed (MissingWindowsTargetTriple) when packaging from a non-Windows host without a triple. Validated by packaging tests (4 pass).

prepare_beta_workspace now takes real_workspace_root and resolves relative beta workspace paths (and the target/ root boundary) against the configured real_workspace_root instead of std::env::current_dir(). Call site updated to pass config.real_workspace_root.
results.notes: beta_workflow integration tests fail at runtime open due to parallel legion-security policy changes (path read denied) - confirmed pre-existing by reproducing identically with the original beta.rs.

results.summary: beta_smoke_command now shell-quotes each path argument via new shell_quote/shell_quote_path helpers (POSIX single-quote, simple args left bare) so paths with spaces/metacharacters yield a re-runnable command.

Renderer smoke status now gates Passed on more than frame_count: added fail-closed gate checks via push_adapter_gate for clipboard/ime/file_dialog adapter paths (Some(false)/None -> error) and for unobserved focus and invalid/unobserved high-DPI scale. Status is Passed only when no gate errors. Validated platform_smoke tests still pass (they construct reports directly).
SmokeObservations::high_dpi_status now accepts any positive finite scale (including 1.0) for the os-observed branch, matching the platform.rs fix.
smoke_command now shell-quotes workspace/file/evidence/session-state/diagnostics-export arguments via local shell_quote/shell_quote_path helpers, so paths with spaces/metacharacters round-trip.

Added normalized_text_range helper and applied it at the drag SetSelection emit site (view.rs ~1930) so backwards drags no longer store an inverted range. drag_selection_range itself was left unchanged because tests/projection_rendering.rs:1532 asserts its raw (un-normalized) fallback output, which is out of scope to edit.
results.notes: Normalization applied at the emit site rather than inside drag_selection_range to avoid breaking an out-of-scope unit test that asserts the raw range.

results.summary: render_fleet_canvas: the unbound 'Pause Workflow' and 'Add Constraint' buttons are now rendered disabled (add_enabled_ui(false) with on_disabled_hover_text) instead of live buttons whose clicks are dropped, and the hard-coded 'confidence 87%' literal was removed (no projected confidence field exists).

render_delegated_hunk_review_controls now wraps all reviews and all hunks in a bounded vertical ScrollArea (max 280px) with no take() caps, so every review/hunk is reachable for accept/reject/pending instead of being silently truncated at 4 reviews / 6 hunks.

git_relative_path now requires a component boundary after strip_prefix (remainder empty, starts with a separator, or root ends with a separator) before accepting, so root /repo no longer matches /repo2/src/lib.rs.

Privacy inspector record rendering now stably partitions records so denied/fully-redacted/external-egress/high-risk records sort first before take(10), and pushes an explicit 'privacy records omitted from preview: N' row when records exceed the cap, so sensitive records beyond index 10 are no longer invisible.

Fixed the gating logic in assistant_rail.rs: apply_as_proposal_available now requires `proposal_id.is_some() && complete`, and render_code_block only renders the Apply-as-proposal button when complete && apply_as_proposal_available (else shows 'proposal unavailable'). Could NOT wire the module: it is orphaned (view.rs declares only mod code_canvas_painter) and depends on legion_ai which is not a dependency of legion-desktop, so adding `pub mod assistant_rail` requires editing Cargo.toml (out of scope). The module is therefore not compiled and the fix cannot be verified by build/clippy.
results.notes: Source defect fixed for if/when the module is wired. Wiring needs a legion_ai dependency (Cargo.toml) which is out of scope; tests/assistant_rail.rs is pre-existing non-compiling because the module is not exported. The in-module #[cfg(test)] assertion (complete=true) remains satisfied.

render_proposal_cards (live equivalent of fleet_card) now gates Approve/Reject on lifecycle: only Created/Validated/Previewed proposals expose enabled Approve/Reject (others render disabled via add_enabled_ui), and the lifecycle state label is surfaced on each card so terminal/applied/denied proposals no longer present active controls.
render_proposal_cards now renders an explicit '{N} more proposals' overflow label after the take(4) loop, counting both rows beyond the cap and the projection's omitted_row_count.

crates/legion-desktop/src/view/manifest_panel.rs does not exist and the cited 'no context items projected before invocation' misreport is nowhere in source. The live trust_rows code simply omits the line when items are empty (no misreport), so the specific described defect is not present.

The live provider rendering already uses authoritative projection fields (availability/class/risk/privacy/refusal reason_code) and does not re-derive policy labels from hard-coded class strings, so the described defect is absent.

crates/legion-desktop/src/view/worker_panel.rs does not exist; no code parses display_safe_labels for a 'signoff:' prefix or constructs sign-off recovery actions from label text. display_safe_labels is only joined into a display string.
